exemplar.title: Determining the volume of a guitar body using mathematical modelling and calculus.

detailedExemplarData.title: Determining the volume of a guitar body using mathematical modelling and calculus.

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

versions.error: Durable Object reset because its code was updated.

strengths: Exceptionally clear structure with a detailed table of contents and numbered headings, facilitating navigation.
Highly coherent narrative: logical progression from introduction through modelling, integration and conclusion.
Concise use of content with minimal irrelevant material; almost all sections directly support the problem investigation.
weaknesses: Occasional awkward phrasing and minor jumps in explanation (e.g. sudden interiorâwaist calculation).
Some redundancy in screenshots and overâexplanation of elementary notation.
Inconsistent citation and bullet formatting detract slightly from polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although coherence and conciseness are not flawless, the exploration is exceptionally well organized and logically developed. The clear introduction, table of contents and distinct subsections meet the highest band descriptor for Presentation.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of integral notation, domain restrictions and units throughout.
Effective integration of multiple representations (graphs, tables, photos with overlays) to support each step.
Logical flow in explanations, with clear justifications for modelling choices and solution steps.
weaknesses: Minor typographical slip (one missing âdxâ) and raw footnote tags affect polish.
Some figures are low resolution or lack axis labels, reducing clarity.
Omission of a coordinateâmapping table for the image alignment step.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used consistently, multiple representations are integrated effectively, and the overall communication is very clear. These qualities justify a top-band award under Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Authentic personal motivation (selecting a new guitar) rooted throughout the exploration.
Clear personal approach in designing bespoke data-collection and modelling methods.
weaknesses: Modelling techniques (quadratics, integration) follow standard syllabus methods without novel extension.
Creativity is solid but does not reach exceptional or highly innovative levels.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, aligning with the descriptor for 2 marks in Personal Engagement. However, it lacks the novelty and innovation to merit the highest band.

strengths: Thoughtful analysis of measurement uncertainties and modelling simplifications.
Meaningful evaluation of results with discussion of potential refinements (e.g. 3D scanning).
weaknesses: Reflection is clustered in a single section rather than woven throughout.
No quantitative error bounds or detailed futureâwork plan provided.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is meaningful and includes thoughtful critique of assumptions and errors, but it is not deep or pervasive enough for the top band. The work aligns with the 2-mark descriptor.

strengths: All mathematical procedures (curve-fitting, definite integration, unit conversion) are relevant and correctly applied.
Mathematics used is fully commensurate with SL level and accurately executed.
Clear demonstration of iterative refinement (adjusting âaâ values) shows solid understanding.
weaknesses: Narrow calculus toolkit; no extension beyond basic integration techniques.
Minor unexplained omission of sound-hole volume from total.
Justification for choosing a parabola over other shapes is not provided numerically.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant, appropriate for the SL course and executed with minor errors. While not comprehensive or expert enough for the top two bands, it clearly meets the criteria for 4 marks in Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within each procedure the student explains what is being done and why (e.g. choice of quadratic transformations, symmetry argument before doubling area).  The logical chain from data to final answer is clear.  Occasional small gaps (no justification for ignoring sound-hole volume) prevent the âexcellentâ standard, so a solid 3-mark level is appropriate.

Derivations of each definite integral, correct unit conversions and sensible final volume indicate good understanding; minor slips (e.g. unexplained neglect of sound-hole volume) exist but do not materially affect correctness.  Thus the work meets âgood understanding and mostly correctâ, the 4-mark band; it falls short of the flawless accuracy needed for 5 or 6 marks.

Graphs, photographs with measurement overlays, tables of functions and areas, and algebraic equations are integrated effectively.  These representations complement one another and help the reader visualise each step.  A few graphs are small or blurred, preventing the highest standard, so the work is best described as âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ, placing it in the 3-mark band.

The investigation follows a clear narrative: introduction of the problem, modelling, integration, evaluation and conclusion. Transitions between subsections (e.g. from modelling to integration) are logical and easy to follow, and no section feels misplaced.  Minor awkward wording occasionally interrupts flow and there are a few jumps in explanation (e.g. sudden introduction of interiorâwaist calculation), so the coherence is strong rather than flawless; this aligns best with the 3-mark descriptor rather than the exemplary 4-mark level.

Using digital imaging, function transformations and integral calculus shows good initiative, yet all techniques are standard syllabus tools; ideas such as spline curves are mentioned only in hindsight.  Hence the work fits the 2-mark description of "good level of creativity and initiative" rather than the exceptional 3-mark level.

The evaluation discusses sources of error, assumptions (uniform thickness, neglecting bracing) and suggests more sophisticated modelling; this represents thoughtful critique of results and process.  Depth is solid but not deeply technical, aligning with the 2-mark band for âthoughtful analysisâ.

Outcomes are compared qualitatively with expected guitar volumes and possible experimental validation is proposed.  The student does not quantify error bounds or plan specific future investigations in detail, so the strand meets "clear evaluation" (2 marks) rather than the comprehensive 3-mark descriptor.

The student reflects on measurement uncertainty, modelling simplifications and potential use of 3D scanning, demonstrating understanding of strengths and weaknesses. Reflection appears in a dedicated section but is not pervasive throughout, so it is "meaningful" but not "substantial", corresponding to 2 marks.

The student uses correct integral notation, domain restrictions, units and transformation symbols almost throughout; occasional typographical slips (missing \;dx in one formula, inconsistent bold bullets) are minor.  Taken together, usage is âmostly consistentâ, satisfying the 3-mark level.

The exploration is clearly rooted in the studentâs personal situation (selecting a new guitar) and the entire modelling strategy is tailored to that context.  However, the approachâoverlaying a photo on Desmos and using quadraticsâis not especially innovative, so the descriptor of a "clear personal approach" (2 marks) is most accurate.

Most of the content directly supports the volume problem; lengthy screen-shots and repeated explanations of basic integral notation add some redundancy, and paragraphs occasionally over-explain elementary ideas, so the exploration is not fully concise.  Nonetheless irrelevant digressions are rare and the majority of text is purposeful, matching the 3-mark description of "concise with mostly relevant content" rather than the flawless concision required for 4 marks.

All mathematicsâfunction transformations, definite integration, cumulative areas, volume calculationâis relevant to the aim and applied appropriately.  While comprehensive relative to the task, methods remain within a narrow calculus toolkit, so the work is âused effectivelyâ rather than âcomprehensivelyâ or âexpertlyâ, fitting the 4-mark band.

Choosing a personal guitar, designing the data-collection method and creating bespoke function fits all demonstrate noticeable independent thinking beyond textbook examples, but the mathematics itself follows a standard integration approach without novel techniques.  This aligns with âsignificant evidenceâ rather than âoutstandingâ, earning 2 marks.

The report opens with a labelled title page, a detailed table of contents, then proceeds through numbered headings (2.1, 3.2, etc.) ending with bibliography.  Each major part (introduction, investigation, evaluation, conclusion) is distinct, and sub-headings further structure the work.  Because the structure is explicit and consistently applied, it meets the descriptor of "exceptionally well organised with clear introduction, body and conclusion" and therefore merits the top band.

Quadratic modelling and single-variable integration are fully commensurate with AA-SL expectations; no material lies beneath or above the course level.  Given appropriate application without extension to HL content, the 4-mark descriptor (âfully commensurate with course levelâ) is most fitting.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and directly indicates the mathematical focus of the exploration, establishing a professional first impression.
Consider adding the student name, candidate number and date on the title page to meet IB presentation requirements and provide full context.
A concise research question or hypothesis would help clarify the aim from the outset; consider stating it explicitly on the title page.
The table of contents is comprehensive and mirrors the report structure accurately, facilitating navigation.
Page numbers are listed but the right column is blank; include corresponding page references for each entry to complete the TOC.
Consider shortening TOC descriptions (e.g. â3. INVESTIGATIONâ), as the table is slightly verbose and could be more concise.
The personal engagement narrative is vivid and shows authentic motivation, strengthening the explorationâs relevance.
The footnote formatting appears as raw tags (âlt;sup>â); ensure proper superscript styling for readability.
Consider condensing the personal engagement section by focusing on key details to improve conciseness and maintain relevance.
The aim is clearly stated in list form, outlining the methodology succinctly.
The narrative describing tool selection contains run-on sentences; break into shorter sentences to enhance clarity.
Consider explicitly connecting the aim to the research question you noted earlier to strengthen logical development.
Figure 1 clearly demonstrates alignment and symmetry checking, aiding understanding of the modelling technique.
The resolution of Figure 2 is low, making measurement details hard to read; consider higher-quality images.
The relationship between photograph scaling and axis units is well explained, showing effective mapping of physical to mathematical space.
Unit conversion steps for subtracting wood thickness are clearly shown, demonstrating appropriate use of mathematics.
The description of choosing positive and negative axes is clear, but consider adding a small diagram in text to clarify for the reader.
The footnote citation style is inconsistent with MLA; standardize formatting across references.
The algebraic manipulation for calculating half the inside waist width is correct and well communicated with LaTeX notation.
The text describes shifting the photo but omits a step-by-step coordinate mapping table; include one for mathematical clarity.
Figure 4 supports the alignment discussion but could be annotated with axis labels and units directly on the image.
The general form $$y=a f(x-h)+k$$ is introduced clearly, aiding understanding of transformations.
The bullet list describing parameters a, h, k is helpful, but inconsistent bullet symbols distract; unify formatting.
It would strengthen the logic to explain why a parabola is chosen over other base functions (e.g. circle segments) for initial fitting.
The progression from testing $$f_1(x)=x^2+11.87$$ to $$0.07x^2+11.87$$ shows iterative refinement and effective use of parameter adjustment.
The choice of scale factor $$a=0.07$$ is not justified numerically; include residual/error measures or target coordinates.
Including a small table of adjusted a-values and resulting maximum deviation would enhance mathematical communication.
